title: Robot snakes searched for Venezuela earthquake survivors in collapsed buildings
excerpt: US robotics researchers flew to Venezuela with snakebots after getting a call.
content_html:
Snakebots lend a figurative helping hand

US robotics researchers flew to Venezuela with snakebots after getting a call.

Search and rescue team members hold Carnegie Mellon University's snake robot while standing in the debris of a collapsed building in Venezuela. Credit: Carnegie Mellon University

Robots slithered like snakes into the rubble of collapsed buildings to search for trapped survivors in the aftermath of Venezuela’s double earthquake disaster.

The so-called snake robots represented a unique technological aid for international search and rescue efforts following two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, killing more than 5,000 people and leaving behind 16,740 injured. Rescuers from around the world used trained dogs, seismic and acoustic listening devices, video cameras on sticks, or thermal imaging cameras to search for signs of survivors—but the snakebots equipped with a video camera could squeeze through tight spaces to search deep within fallen building debris.

“The reason why snake robots are useful is they can get into tightly packed volumes that machinery and people otherwise cannot, and that’s important in search and rescue because we were able to extend the visual reach of the rescue workers in a safe way,” Howie Choset, head of the Biorobotics Lab at Carnegie Mellon University in Pittsburgh, told Ars. “In a sense, you’re doing minimally invasive surgery on a structure with a snake robot.”

Each 4-foot-long snakebot is modular in design with individual body segments that can be swapped out and easily replaced or upgraded. A human operator directs the robot using a video game controller and a control case laptop tethered to the robot, but software algorithms allow the robot to autonomously coordinate the body segments for the appropriate body movements when being directed to travel from point A to B.

Choset and his colleagues have spent nearly two decades developing different versions of the snake robots that can explore archaeological sites, swim underwater and even assist with minimally invasive surgery. But they first put the snake robot to the test in search and rescue scenarios following an earthquake that struck Mexico City on September 19, 2017, when they helped search a collapsed apartment building.

An unexpected journey

That first snake robot deployment caught the attention of Beatriz Gonzalez, a native Venezuelan living in Atlanta, when she asked the AI chatbot Grok for ways to help search and rescue operations in Venezuela following the earthquakes, according to The Tribune-Review. Grok referred her to a Carnegie Mellon University article about the 2017 operation in Mexico City.

Gonzalez called Choset’s lab. The robotics researchers were eager to help and eventually confirmed they would go while onstage preparing to demonstrate their robots during a concert celebrating America’s 250th anniversary at Pittsburgh’s Point State Park on June 27. “We were on between Third Eye Blind and Nelly the rapper,” Choset said.

That decision led to frenzied overnight packing and work to prepare the robots for the work that lay ahead. The researchers had to replace head-mounted cameras on several robots to ensure they were working properly, and they packed extra batteries for the control case that also serves as the robot’s power supply. Within 24 hours, they were flying to Miami with three snake robots in tow on their way to Venezuela.

The stopover in Miami on June 28 took slightly longer than expected because the team scrambled to find a private jet owner willing to take them to Venezuela. A few jet owners backed out because of restrictions imposed by the Venezuelan government. But Gonzalez assisted the team by using her connections, and they eventually got a flight.

The search for survivors

Once the plane landed in Venezuela on June 30, the team was greeted by US Marines before being handed over to the Venezuelan Red Cross to make their way to the hard-hit city of La Guaira in the northern part of the country. The researchers joined international teams from many different countries in searching several sites of collapsed buildings over the course of three days from June 30 to July 3.

In one case, a Jordanian rescue team with a dog had concluded that a collapsed apartment building had no survivors, whereas a Colombian team’s infrared cameras seemed to indicate the possible presence of a survivor. The Carnegie Mellon team deployed a snakebot into a small hole, while a Venezuelan father hovered nearby to see if the team could find his little boy beneath the rubble.

The snake robot went in about 15 to 20 feet without finding anybody. But the man whose son was missing still expressed his thanks to the robotics researchers for trying. “The people in Venezuela were beyond gracious,” Choset told Ars.

Choset also recalled local Venezuelans continually supplying the team with hot meals, along with handing them sports drinks and even a pound cake while they were assisting with search efforts.

“They were very caring, and they’re showing this kind of love, this kind of affection for us while they’re suffering a burden that no human should ever have to suffer,” Choset said.

The United Nations has requested nearly $300 million to help more than 1.3 million people affected by the earthquake, and the World Bank has put the cost of earthquake damage to buildings at $19.6 billion.

Incidentally, the Trump administration has collected more than $13 billion in revenues from Venezuelan oil exports and sales, according to calculations by the Financial Times. The United States seized control of Venezuela’s oil revenues after capturing President Nicolás Maduro in a military raid in January and securing the cooperation of vice president Delcy Rodríguez.

That oil revenue could presumably help to rebuild Venezuela. But sluggish economic recovery in the country raises questions about whether all the oil money has been made available for Venezuela’s use, according to the Financial Times. The Trump administration has not clarified how it’s handling the funds despite questions from US lawmakers.

The return and plans for the future

The snakebot team eventually had to return to the United States without finding a survivor. But nearly 6,500 people were rescued with the help of search and rescue teams from all over the world.

By coincidence, Choset ended up having a lengthy conversation with a woman whose husband, a security guard, was buried for eight days in the collapsed basement of a shopping center. He described himself as overjoyed when he later learned that the woman’s husband had been pulled out alive.

“She had two kids, and the age difference was the same as my two boys, so it hit home for me there,” Choset told Ars.

The Carnegie Mellon researchers have several ideas for how to improve the snake robot for possible future deployments. One of their priorities is to attach light-based sensors that can help perform 3D point cloud mapping of a snakebot’s environment—giving the robot operator and search and rescue teams a better sense of how to steer the robot beneath collapsed buildings.

Following their return, the team members also wrote up notes about their experiences to compile a to-do list for any unexpected journeys in the future. “The big thing is what to pack and how to pack,” Choset said.

The snake robots are still very much research prototypes rather than operational tools for search and rescue. But Choset credited his team’s prior experiences and training on search and rescue scenarios using the snakebots, which included cycles of breaking and fixing the robots to improve them. He also highlighted his lab’s philosophy of taking the time and energy to build robust robots capable of working reliably beyond a few research demonstrations.

“My students and staff took the attitude of, ‘Let’s make sure these are robust systems as much as possible,’” Choset said. “So that when we’re doing our research, we’re not constantly fighting with the robot to get things to work.”

Jeremy Hsu is a reporter exploring a wide range of topics across deep tech and AI. He has previously written for New Scientist, Scientific American, IEEE Spectrum, Wired, Undark Magazine and MIT Tech Review, among many other publications, about topics such as deepfakes, data centers, drones, battery tech, robotics, and GPS jamming. He also has a Master of Arts in Journalism from NYU, and a bachelor's degree from University of Pennsylvania in History and Sociology of Science, with a minor in English.

sentences.zh: 美国机器人研究人员接到电话后带着蛇形机器人飞往委内瑞拉。

blocks.html_en: Search and rescue team members hold Carnegie Mellon University's snake robot while standing in the debris of a collapsed building in Venezuela. Credit: Carnegie Mellon University

sentences.en: Search and rescue team members hold Carnegie Mellon University's snake robot while standing in the debris of a collapsed building in Venezuela.
sentences.zh: 搜救队成员站在委内瑞拉一座倒塌建筑的废墟中，手持卡内基梅隆大学的蛇形机器人。

sentences.en: Credit: Carnegie Mellon University
sentences.zh: 图片来源：卡内基梅隆大学

blocks.html_en: Robots slithered like snakes into the rubble of collapsed buildings to search for trapped survivors in the aftermath of Venezuela’s double earthquake disaster.

sentences.en: Robots slithered like snakes into the rubble of collapsed buildings to search for trapped survivors in the aftermath of Venezuela’s double earthquake disaster.
sentences.zh: 在委内瑞拉两次地震灾难之后，机器人像蛇一样爬进倒塌建筑物的废墟中寻找被困的幸存者。

blocks.html_en: The so-called snake robots represented a unique technological aid for international search and rescue efforts following two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, killing more than 5,000 people and leaving behind 16,740 injured. Rescuers from around the world used trained dogs, seismic and acoustic listening devices, video cameras on sticks, or thermal imaging cameras to search for signs of survivors—but the snakebots equipped with a video camera could squeeze through tight spaces to search deep within fallen building debris.

sentences.en: The so-called snake robots represented a unique technological aid for international search and rescue efforts following two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, killing more than 5,000 people and leaving behind 16,740 injured.
sentences.zh: 6 月 24 日委内瑞拉发生两次地震，造成 5,000 多人死亡，16,740 人受伤，之后，所谓的蛇形机器人为国际搜救工作提供了独特的技术援助。

sentences.en: Rescuers from around the world used trained dogs, seismic and acoustic listening devices, video cameras on sticks, or thermal imaging cameras to search for signs of survivors—but the snakebots equipped with a video camera could squeeze through tight spaces to search deep within fallen building debris.
sentences.zh: 来自世界各地的救援人员使用训练有素的狗、地震和声学监听设备、棍子上的摄像机或热成像摄像机来寻找幸存者的迹象，但配备摄像机的蛇形机器人可以挤过狭窄的空间，在倒塌的建筑废墟中进行深入搜索。

blocks.html_en: “The reason why snake robots are useful is they can get into tightly packed volumes that machinery and people otherwise cannot, and that’s important in search and rescue because we were able to extend the visual reach of the rescue workers in a safe way,” Howie Choset, head of the Biorobotics Lab at Carnegie Mellon University in Pittsburgh, told Ars. “In a sense, you’re doing minimally invasive surgery on a structure with a snake robot.”

sentences.en: “The reason why snake robots are useful is they can get into tightly packed volumes that machinery and people otherwise cannot, and that’s important in search and rescue because we were able to extend the visual reach of the rescue workers in a safe way,” Howie Choset, head of the Biorobotics Lab at Carnegie Mellon University in Pittsburgh, told Ars.
sentences.zh: 匹兹堡卡内基梅隆大学生物机器人实验室负责人豪伊·乔塞特 (Howie Choset) 表示：“蛇形机器人之所以有用，是因为它们可以进入机器和人员无法进入的紧密空间，这对于搜救来说非常重要，因为我们能够以安全的方式扩大救援人员的视觉范围。”

sentences.en: “In a sense, you’re doing minimally invasive surgery on a structure with a snake robot.”
sentences.zh: “从某种意义上说，你正在用蛇形机器人对一个结构进行微创手术。”

A human operator directs the robot using a video game controller and a control case laptop tethered to the robot, but software algorithms allow the robot to autonomously coordinate the body segments for the appropriate body movements when being directed to travel from point A to B.

blocks.html_en: Choset and his colleagues have spent nearly two decades developing different versions of the snake robots that can explore archaeological sites, swim underwater and even assist with minimally invasive surgery. But they first put the snake robot to the test in search and rescue scenarios following an earthquake that struck Mexico City on September 19, 2017, when they helped search a collapsed apartment building.

sentences.zh: Choset 和他的同事花了近二十年的时间开发不同版本的蛇形机器人，这些机器人可以探索考古遗址、在水下游泳，甚至协助微创手术。

sentences.en: But they first put the snake robot to the test in search and rescue scenarios following an earthquake that struck Mexico City on September 19, 2017, when they helped search a collapsed apartment building.
sentences.zh: 但在 2017 年 9 月 19 日墨西哥城发生地震后，他们首先在搜救场景中对蛇形机器人进行了测试，当时他们帮助搜索了一栋倒塌的公寓楼。

sentences.zh: 6月30日，飞机降落在委内瑞拉后，该团队受到了美国海军陆战队的迎接，然后被移交给委内瑞拉红十字会，前往该国北部受灾严重的城市拉瓜伊拉。

sentences.en: The researchers joined international teams from many different countries in searching several sites of collapsed buildings over the course of three days from June 30 to July 3.
sentences.zh: 研究人员与来自许多不同国家的国际团队一起，在6月30日至7月3日的三天时间里搜寻了多个倒塌建筑物的地点。

blocks.html_en: In one case, a Jordanian rescue team with a dog had concluded that a collapsed apartment building had no survivors, whereas a Colombian team’s infrared cameras seemed to indicate the possible presence of a survivor. The Carnegie Mellon team deployed a snakebot into a small hole, while a Venezuelan father hovered nearby to see if the team could find his little boy beneath the rubble.

sentences.en: In one case, a Jordanian rescue team with a dog had concluded that a collapsed apartment building had no survivors, whereas a Colombian team’s infrared cameras seemed to indicate the possible presence of a survivor.
sentences.zh: 在一个案例中，一支带着狗的约旦救援队得出的结论是，一栋倒塌的公寓楼没有幸存者，而哥伦比亚救援队的红外摄像机似乎表明可能存在幸存者。

sentences.en: The Carnegie Mellon team deployed a snakebot into a small hole, while a Venezuelan father hovered nearby to see if the team could find his little boy beneath the rubble.
sentences.zh: 卡内基梅隆大学团队在一个小洞中部署了一个蛇形机器人，而一位委内瑞拉父亲则在附近盘旋，看看团队是否能在废墟下找到他的小男孩。

blocks.html_en: The snake robot went in about 15 to 20 feet without finding anybody. But the man whose son was missing still expressed his thanks to the robotics researchers for trying. “The people in Venezuela were beyond gracious,” Choset told Ars.

sentences.en: The snake robot went in about 15 to 20 feet without finding anybody.
sentences.zh: 蛇形机器人深入了大约 15 到 20 英尺，没有发现任何人。

sentences.en: But the man whose son was missing still expressed his thanks to the robotics researchers for trying.
sentences.zh: 但儿子失踪的男子仍然对机器人研究人员的尝试表示感谢。

sentences.en: “The people in Venezuela were beyond gracious,” Choset told Ars.
sentences.zh: “委内瑞拉人民非常仁慈，”乔塞特告诉 Ars。

blocks.html_en: Choset also recalled local Venezuelans continually supplying the team with hot meals, along with handing them sports drinks and even a pound cake while they were assisting with search efforts.

sentences.en: Choset also recalled local Venezuelans continually supplying the team with hot meals, along with handing them sports drinks and even a pound cake while they were assisting with search efforts.
sentences.zh: 乔塞特还回忆道，当地的委内瑞拉人在他们协助搜寻工作时，不断为救援队提供热食、运动饮料，甚至一块磅蛋糕。

blocks.html_en: “They were very caring, and they’re showing this kind of love, this kind of affection for us while they’re suffering a burden that no human should ever have to suffer,” Choset said.

sentences.en: “They were very caring, and they’re showing this kind of love, this kind of affection for us while they’re suffering a burden that no human should ever have to suffer,” Choset said.
sentences.zh: 乔塞特说：“他们非常关心我们，当他们承受着人类不应该承受的负担时，他们向我们展示了这种爱和感情。”

blocks.html_en: The open face of a damaged building in the aftermath of two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, 2026.

sentences.en: The open face of a damaged building in the aftermath of two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, 2026.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 6 月 24 日委内瑞拉发生两次地震后，一座受损建筑物的外露面。

blocks.html_en: Credit: Howie Choset

sentences.en: Credit: Howie Choset
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Howie Choset

sentences.en: The open face of a damaged building in the aftermath of two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, 2026.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 6 月 24 日委内瑞拉发生两次地震后，一座受损建筑物的外露面。

sentences.en: Credit: Howie Choset
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Howie Choset

blocks.html_en: The open face of a damaged building in the aftermath of two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, 2026. Credit: Howie Choset

sentences.en: The open face of a damaged building in the aftermath of two earthquakes that struck Venezuela on June 24, 2026.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 6 月 24 日委内瑞拉发生两次地震后，一座受损建筑物的外露面。

sentences.en: Credit: Howie Choset
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Howie Choset

blocks.html_en: The United Nations has requested nearly $300 million to help more than 1.3 million people affected by the earthquake, and the World Bank has put the cost of earthquake damage to buildings at $19.6 billion.

sentences.en: The United Nations has requested nearly $300 million to help more than 1.3 million people affected by the earthquake, and the World Bank has put the cost of earthquake damage to buildings at $19.6 billion.
sentences.zh: 联合国已请求近3亿美元来帮助超过130万受地震影响的人，世界银行估计地震对建筑物造成的损失为196亿美元。

blocks.html_en: Incidentally, the Trump administration has collected more than $13 billion in revenues from Venezuelan oil exports and sales, according to calculations by the Financial Times. The United States seized control of Venezuela’s oil revenues after capturing President Nicolás Maduro in a military raid in January and securing the cooperation of vice president Delcy Rodríguez.

sentences.en: Incidentally, the Trump administration has collected more than $13 billion in revenues from Venezuelan oil exports and sales, according to calculations by the Financial Times.
sentences.zh: 顺便说一句，根据英国《金融时报》的计算，特朗普政府从委内瑞拉石油出口和销售中获得了超过130亿美元的收入。

sentences.en: The United States seized control of Venezuela’s oil revenues after capturing President Nicolás Maduro in a military raid in January and securing the cooperation of vice president Delcy Rodríguez.
sentences.zh: 美国在一月份的军事袭击中抓获总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗并获得副总统德尔西·罗德里格斯的合作后，控制了委内瑞拉的石油收入。

blocks.html_en: That oil revenue could presumably help to rebuild Venezuela. But sluggish economic recovery in the country raises questions about whether all the oil money has been made available for Venezuela’s use, according to the Financial Times. The Trump administration has not clarified how it’s handling the funds despite questions from US lawmakers.

sentences.en: That oil revenue could presumably help to rebuild Venezuela.
sentences.zh: 这些石油收入可能有助于委内瑞拉的重建。

sentences.en: But sluggish economic recovery in the country raises questions about whether all the oil money has been made available for Venezuela’s use, according to the Financial Times.
sentences.zh: 但据英国《金融时报》报道，该国经济复苏缓慢引发了人们的疑问：所有石油资金是否都已可供委内瑞拉使用。

sentences.en: The Trump administration has not clarified how it’s handling the funds despite questions from US lawmakers.
sentences.zh: 尽管美国立法者提出质疑，特朗普政府尚未澄清如何处理这些资金。

blocks.html_en: The return and plans for the future

sentences.en: The return and plans for the future
sentences.zh: 回报及未来计划

blocks.html_en: The snakebot team eventually had to return to the United States without finding a survivor. But nearly 6,500 people were rescued with the help of search and rescue teams from all over the world.

sentences.en: The snakebot team eventually had to return to the United States without finding a survivor.
sentences.zh: 蛇形机器人小组最终不得不返回美国，没有找到幸存者。

sentences.en: But nearly 6,500 people were rescued with the help of search and rescue teams from all over the world.
sentences.zh: 但在世界各地搜救队的帮助下，近6500人获救。

blocks.html_en: By coincidence, Choset ended up having a lengthy conversation with a woman whose husband, a security guard, was buried for eight days in the collapsed basement of a shopping center. He described himself as overjoyed when he later learned that the woman’s husband had been pulled out alive.

sentences.en: By coincidence, Choset ended up having a lengthy conversation with a woman whose husband, a security guard, was buried for eight days in the collapsed basement of a shopping center.
sentences.zh: 巧合的是，乔塞特最终与一位女士进行了长时间的交谈，她的丈夫是一名保安，在一家购物中心倒塌的地下室里被埋了八天。

sentences.en: He described himself as overjoyed when he later learned that the woman’s husband had been pulled out alive.
sentences.zh: 当他后来得知该女子的丈夫被活着救出时，他形容自己欣喜若狂。

blocks.html_en: “She had two kids, and the age difference was the same as my two boys, so it hit home for me there,” Choset told Ars.

sentences.en: “She had two kids, and the age difference was the same as my two boys, so it hit home for me there,” Choset told Ars.
sentences.zh: “她有两个孩子，而且年龄差和我的两个儿子一样，所以这对我来说很重要，”乔塞特告诉 Ars。

blocks.html_en: The Carnegie Mellon researchers have several ideas for how to improve the snake robot for possible future deployments. One of their priorities is to attach light-based sensors that can help perform 3D point cloud mapping of a snakebot’s environment—giving the robot operator and search and rescue teams a better sense of how to steer the robot beneath collapsed buildings.

sentences.en: The Carnegie Mellon researchers have several ideas for how to improve the snake robot for possible future deployments.
sentences.zh: 卡内基梅隆大学的研究人员对于如何改进蛇形机器人以适应未来可能的部署有几个想法。

sentences.en: One of their priorities is to attach light-based sensors that can help perform 3D point cloud mapping of a snakebot’s environment—giving the robot operator and search and rescue teams a better sense of how to steer the robot beneath collapsed buildings.
sentences.zh: 他们的首要任务之一是安装基于光的传感器，帮助对蛇形机器人的环境进行 3D 点云测绘，让机器人操作员和搜救团队更好地了解如何在倒塌的建筑物下引导机器人。

blocks.html_en: Mexican search and rescue workers from the Los Topos volunteer group react to finding four deceased people in collapsed building debris in Venezuela, following two earthquakes that struck on June 24.

sentences.en: Mexican search and rescue workers from the Los Topos volunteer group react to finding four deceased people in collapsed building debris in Venezuela, following two earthquakes that struck on June 24.
sentences.zh: 6 月 24 日发生两次地震后，委内瑞拉 Los Topos 志愿者组织的墨西哥搜救人员在倒塌的建筑废墟中发现了四名死者。

blocks.html_en: Credit: Howie Choset

sentences.en: Credit: Howie Choset
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Howie Choset

sentences.en: Mexican search and rescue workers from the Los Topos volunteer group react to finding four deceased people in collapsed building debris in Venezuela, following two earthquakes that struck on June 24.
sentences.zh: 6 月 24 日发生两次地震后，委内瑞拉 Los Topos 志愿者组织的墨西哥搜救人员在倒塌的建筑废墟中发现了四名死者。

sentences.en: Credit: Howie Choset
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Howie Choset

blocks.html_en: Mexican search and rescue workers from the Los Topos volunteer group react to finding four deceased people in collapsed building debris in Venezuela, following two earthquakes that struck on June 24. Credit: Howie Choset

sentences.en: Mexican search and rescue workers from the Los Topos volunteer group react to finding four deceased people in collapsed building debris in Venezuela, following two earthquakes that struck on June 24.
sentences.zh: 6 月 24 日发生两次地震后，委内瑞拉 Los Topos 志愿者组织的墨西哥搜救人员在倒塌的建筑废墟中发现了四名死者。

sentences.en: Credit: Howie Choset
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Howie Choset

blocks.html_en: Following their return, the team members also wrote up notes about their experiences to compile a to-do list for any unexpected journeys in the future. “The big thing is what to pack and how to pack,” Choset said.

sentences.en: Following their return, the team members also wrote up notes about their experiences to compile a to-do list for any unexpected journeys in the future.
sentences.zh: 回国后，队员们还写下了自己的经历，为未来意外的旅程制定一份待办事项清单。

sentences.en: “The big thing is what to pack and how to pack,” Choset said.
sentences.zh: “最重要的是打包什么以及如何打包，”乔塞特说。

blocks.html_en: The snake robots are still very much research prototypes rather than operational tools for search and rescue. But Choset credited his team’s prior experiences and training on search and rescue scenarios using the snakebots, which included cycles of breaking and fixing the robots to improve them. He also highlighted his lab’s philosophy of taking the time and energy to build robust robots capable of working reliably beyond a few research demonstrations.

sentences.en: The snake robots are still very much research prototypes rather than operational tools for search and rescue.
sentences.zh: 蛇形机器人在很大程度上仍然是研究原型，而不是搜索和救援的操作工具。

sentences.en: But Choset credited his team’s prior experiences and training on search and rescue scenarios using the snakebots, which included cycles of breaking and fixing the robots to improve them.
sentences.zh: 但乔塞特赞扬了他的团队之前使用蛇形机器人进行搜索和救援场景的经验和培训，其中包括对机器人进行破坏和修复以改进它们的循环。

sentences.en: He also highlighted his lab’s philosophy of taking the time and energy to build robust robots capable of working reliably beyond a few research demonstrations.
sentences.zh: 他还强调了他的实验室的理念，即花时间和精力来建造强大的机器人，这些机器人能够在几次研究演示之外可靠地工作。

blocks.html_en: “My students and staff took the attitude of, ‘Let’s make sure these are robust systems as much as possible,’” Choset said. “So that when we’re doing our research, we’re not constantly fighting with the robot to get things to work.”

sentences.en: “My students and staff took the attitude of, ‘Let’s make sure these are robust systems as much as possible,’” Choset said.
sentences.zh: “我的学生和工作人员的态度是，‘让我们尽可能确保这些系统是强大的，’”乔塞特说。

sentences.en: “So that when we’re doing our research, we’re not constantly fighting with the robot to get things to work.”
sentences.zh: “这样，当我们进行研究时，我们就不会为了让事情正常运转而不断地与机器人斗争。”

blocks.html_en: Jeremy Hsu is a reporter exploring a wide range of topics across deep tech and AI. He has previously written for New Scientist, Scientific American, IEEE Spectrum, Wired, Undark Magazine and MIT Tech Review, among many other publications, about topics such as deepfakes, data centers, drones, battery tech, robotics, and GPS jamming. He also has a Master of Arts in Journalism from NYU, and a bachelor's degree from University of Pennsylvania in History and Sociology of Science, with a minor in English.

sentences.en: Jeremy Hsu is a reporter exploring a wide range of topics across deep tech and AI.
sentences.zh: Jeremy Hsu 是一名记者，探索深度科技和人工智能领域的广泛话题。

sentences.en: He has previously written for New Scientist, Scientific American, IEEE Spectrum, Wired, Undark Magazine and MIT Tech Review, among many other publications, about topics such as deepfakes, data centers, drones, battery tech, robotics, and GPS jamming.
sentences.zh: 他此前曾为《新科学家》、《科学美国人》、IEEE Spectrum、《连线》、Undark 杂志和《麻省理工科技评论》等许多出版物撰写文章，主题涉及深度假货、数据中心、无人机、电池技术、机器人和 GPS 干扰等。

sentences.en: He also has a Master of Arts in Journalism from NYU, and a bachelor's degree from University of Pennsylvania in History and Sociology of Science, with a minor in English.
sentences.zh: 他还拥有纽约大学新闻学文学硕士学位，以及宾夕法尼亚大学历史和科学社会学学士学位，辅修英语。

blocks.html_en: 10 Comments

sentences.en: 10 Comments
sentences.zh: 10 条评论

content_html_zh:
蛇形机器人伸出了象征性的帮助之手

美国机器人研究人员接到电话后带着蛇形机器人飞往委内瑞拉。

搜救队成员站在委内瑞拉一座倒塌建筑的废墟中，手持卡内基梅隆大学的蛇形机器人。 图片来源：卡内基梅隆大学

在委内瑞拉两次地震灾难之后，机器人像蛇一样爬进倒塌建筑物的废墟中寻找被困的幸存者。

6 月 24 日委内瑞拉发生两次地震，造成 5,000 多人死亡，16,740 人受伤，之后，所谓的蛇形机器人为国际搜救工作提供了独特的技术援助。 来自世界各地的救援人员使用训练有素的狗、地震和声学监听设备、棍子上的摄像机或热成像摄像机来寻找幸存者的迹象，但配备摄像机的蛇形机器人可以挤过狭窄的空间，在倒塌的建筑废墟中进行深入搜索。

匹兹堡卡内基梅隆大学生物机器人实验室负责人豪伊·乔塞特 (Howie Choset) 表示：“蛇形机器人之所以有用，是因为它们可以进入机器和人员无法进入的紧密空间，这对于搜救来说非常重要，因为我们能够以安全的方式扩大救援人员的视觉范围。” “从某种意义上说，你正在用蛇形机器人对一个结构进行微创手术。”

每个 4 英尺长的蛇形机器人均采用模块化设计，具有可以更换并轻松更换或升级的单独身体部分。 人类操作员使用视频游戏控制器和连接到机器人的控制箱笔记本电脑来引导机器人，但软件算法允许机器人在被引导从 A 点行进到 B 点时自主协调身体部位以进行适当的身体运动。

Choset 和他的同事花了近二十年的时间开发不同版本的蛇形机器人，这些机器人可以探索考古遗址、在水下游泳，甚至协助微创手术。 但在 2017 年 9 月 19 日墨西哥城发生地震后，他们首先在搜救场景中对蛇形机器人进行了测试，当时他们帮助搜索了一栋倒塌的公寓楼。

意想不到的旅程

据《论坛报评论》报道，首次部署蛇形机器人引起了居住在亚特兰大的委内瑞拉人 Beatriz Gonzalez 的注意，当时她向人工智能聊天机器人 Grok 询问如何帮助委内瑞拉地震后的搜救行动。 Grok 向她推荐了卡内基梅隆大学一篇关于 2017 年墨西哥城行动的文章。

冈萨雷斯给乔塞特的实验室打了电话。 机器人研究人员渴望提供帮助，并最终确认他们将在 6 月 27 日在匹兹堡 Point 州立公园举行的庆祝美国成立 250 周年的音乐会上上台准备展示他们的机器人。 “我们在第三眼盲人和说唱歌手耐莉之间进行，”乔塞特说。

这一决定导致了连夜疯狂的打包工作，并为机器人为即将到来的工作做好准备。 研究人员必须更换几个机器人上的头戴式摄像头，以确保它们正常工作，并且他们为控制箱配备了额外的电池，该控制箱也充当机器人的电源。 24小时内，他们带着三台蛇形机器人飞往迈阿密，前往委内瑞拉。

6月28日在迈阿密中途停留的时间比预期稍长，因为团队急于寻找愿意带他们前往委内瑞拉的私人飞机所有者。 由于委内瑞拉政府施加的限制，一些飞机所有者退出了。 但冈萨雷斯利用她的人脉帮助团队，最终他们得到了航班。

搜寻幸存者

6月30日，飞机降落在委内瑞拉后，该团队受到了美国海军陆战队的迎接，然后被移交给委内瑞拉红十字会，前往该国北部受灾严重的城市拉瓜伊拉。 研究人员与来自许多不同国家的国际团队一起，在6月30日至7月3日的三天时间里搜寻了多个倒塌建筑物的地点。

在一个案例中，一支带着狗的约旦救援队得出的结论是，一栋倒塌的公寓楼没有幸存者，而哥伦比亚救援队的红外摄像机似乎表明可能存在幸存者。 卡内基梅隆大学团队在一个小洞中部署了一个蛇形机器人，而一位委内瑞拉父亲则在附近盘旋，看看团队是否能在废墟下找到他的小男孩。

蛇形机器人深入了大约 15 到 20 英尺，没有发现任何人。 但儿子失踪的男子仍然对机器人研究人员的尝试表示感谢。 “委内瑞拉人民非常仁慈，”乔塞特告诉 Ars。

乔塞特还回忆道，当地的委内瑞拉人在他们协助搜寻工作时，不断为救援队提供热食、运动饮料，甚至一块磅蛋糕。

乔塞特说：“他们非常关心我们，当他们承受着人类不应该承受的负担时，他们向我们展示了这种爱和感情。”

联合国已请求近3亿美元来帮助超过130万受地震影响的人，世界银行估计地震对建筑物造成的损失为196亿美元。

顺便说一句，根据英国《金融时报》的计算，特朗普政府从委内瑞拉石油出口和销售中获得了超过130亿美元的收入。 美国在一月份的军事袭击中抓获总统尼古拉斯·马杜罗并获得副总统德尔西·罗德里格斯的合作后，控制了委内瑞拉的石油收入。

这些石油收入可能有助于委内瑞拉的重建。 但据英国《金融时报》报道，该国经济复苏缓慢引发了人们的疑问：所有石油资金是否都已可供委内瑞拉使用。 尽管美国立法者提出质疑，特朗普政府尚未澄清如何处理这些资金。

回报及未来计划

蛇形机器人小组最终不得不返回美国，没有找到幸存者。 但在世界各地搜救队的帮助下，近6500人获救。

巧合的是，乔塞特最终与一位女士进行了长时间的交谈，她的丈夫是一名保安，在一家购物中心倒塌的地下室里被埋了八天。 当他后来得知该女子的丈夫被活着救出时，他形容自己欣喜若狂。

“她有两个孩子，而且年龄差和我的两个儿子一样，所以这对我来说很重要，”乔塞特告诉 Ars。

卡内基梅隆大学的研究人员对于如何改进蛇形机器人以适应未来可能的部署有几个想法。 他们的首要任务之一是安装基于光的传感器，帮助对蛇形机器人的环境进行 3D 点云测绘，让机器人操作员和搜救团队更好地了解如何在倒塌的建筑物下引导机器人。

回国后，队员们还写下了自己的经历，为未来意外的旅程制定一份待办事项清单。 “最重要的是打包什么以及如何打包，”乔塞特说。

蛇形机器人在很大程度上仍然是研究原型，而不是搜索和救援的操作工具。 但乔塞特赞扬了他的团队之前使用蛇形机器人进行搜索和救援场景的经验和培训，其中包括对机器人进行破坏和修复以改进它们的循环。 他还强调了他的实验室的理念，即花时间和精力来建造强大的机器人，这些机器人能够在几次研究演示之外可靠地工作。

“我的学生和工作人员的态度是，‘让我们尽可能确保这些系统是强大的，’”乔塞特说。 “这样，当我们进行研究时，我们就不会为了让事情正常运转而不断地与机器人斗争。”

Jeremy Hsu 是一名记者，探索深度科技和人工智能领域的广泛话题。 他此前曾为《新科学家》、《科学美国人》、IEEE Spectrum、《连线》、Undark 杂志和《麻省理工科技评论》等许多出版物撰写文章，主题涉及深度假货、数据中心、无人机、电池技术、机器人和 GPS 干扰等。 他还拥有纽约大学新闻学文学硕士学位，以及宾夕法尼亚大学历史和科学社会学学士学位，辅修英语。
